waitPolicy.variableWritePolicy: Generated LS must explicitly write the controller wait-timeout variable immediately before each WAIT ... TIMEOUT instruction so the 3-minute policy is visible and easy to tune after observation.
waitPolicy.onTimeoutDefault: Preserve WIP flags, stop infeed, set R[80:A_CELL_STATE]=900, set R[90:A_STEP_STATUS] to the step-specific timeout code, and raise a specific alarm/status path.

wipRepresentation.description: R[80] represents lifecycle mode only. Physical WIP is represented by existing part-location flags because the cell can be partially or fully populated.

signals.meaning: A picked part is staged for CNC load.
A part is currently in the CNC.
A part has been unloaded from CNC and is staged for tube insertion.
A part is currently in the tube insertion station.
A part has been unloaded from tube insertion and is staged for conveyor/drop.

states.name: EMPTY_IDLE
states.description: Cold start or completed state with no active WIP flags set.

states.name: FILLING
states.description: Infeed is enabled and the pipeline is being populated from an empty or partially populated state.

states.name: RUNNING_PIPELINE
states.description: Normal production with one or more WIP flags active; the pipeline may become fully populated.

states.name: DRAINING
states.description: Infeed is off, but existing WIP remains and must continue through CNC, tube insertion, and conveyor/drop.

states.name: FINISHED
states.description: Infeed is off and all WIP flags are clear after the remaining pipeline parts have been processed.

states.name: FAULTED
states.description: A_MAIN stopped by an explicit alarm/failure path with lifecycle mode and WIP flags preserved for operator recovery.

transitionExamples.name: cold-start-empty
transitionExamples.wipCondition: F[61], F[62], F[63], F[64], and F[65] are all OFF.
transitionExamples.description: Robot can start from a genuinely empty cell without pretending a part is already assigned to CNC or tube insertion.

transitionExamples.name: pipeline-fill
transitionExamples.wipCondition: One or more WIP flags become ON as parts are picked, loaded into CNC, moved to tube insertion, and staged for conveyor/drop.
transitionExamples.description: The cell transitions from empty to partially populated and then normal pipelined production without encoding part location into R[80].

transitionExamples.name: fully-populated-running
transitionExamples.wipCondition: Multiple WIP flags may be ON at the same time, including CNC and tube insertion occupancy.
transitionExamples.description: Fully populated is treated as a derived WIP condition, not a separate lifecycle state, because station occupancy is already represented by the flags.

transitionExamples.name: finish-drain
transitionExamples.wipCondition: F[60:INFEED] is OFF; existing WIP flags continue through CNC, tube insertion, and conveyor/drop until all WIP flags are OFF.
transitionExamples.description: The finish path stops adding parts but continues processing loaded parts before declaring the workflow finished.

steps.name: START_CHECKS
steps.intent: Verify home/perch, counter, and CNC alarm conditions before any cycle work.
preconditions: F_ baseline remains available for fallback.
successCriteria: Robot is at a permitted start condition.
CNC alarm input is not active.
steps.failureBehavior: Raise the corresponding user alarm, set state FAULTED, preserve WIP flags, and do not enter the cycle loop.

steps.name: INITIALIZE
steps.intent: Initialize existing cell state and start feeder/vision support behavior.
actions: Start or validate feeder/vision async task.
successCriteria: Async feeder/vision ownership is known before entering the main loop.
steps.failureBehavior: Set state FAULTED and do not continue into main loop.

steps.name: GET_PART
steps.intent: Go home, wait for a feeder/vision part-ready signal, pick the part, optionally regrip, and mark it ready for CNC.
preconditions: Lifecycle state is FILLING or RUNNING_PIPELINE with infeed enabled.
actions: Call F_CALC_REGRIP and F_REGRIP if required by part/pattern.
successCriteria: Counter behavior is applied without losing WIP.
steps.failureBehavior: If STOP is requested, stop infeed and loop without changing WIP. If a step fails, set state FAULTED and preserve the last known WIP state.

externalWaits.expected: Part ready ON or STOP ON
externalWaits.onTimeout: Apply global timeout policy. Stop infeed, preserve WIP flags, set lifecycle FAULTED, and report feeder/vision part-ready timeout.

steps.name: CNC_EXCHANGE
steps.intent: When CNC is ready, enter CNC, unload any finished part, load any waiting part, acknowledge CNC, and exit.
preconditions: CNC Sync1 signal is expected to represent a safe process-ready condition.
actions: If part is in CNC, call F_UNLOAD_CNC and transition to part-for-tube-insertion.
If part is for CNC, call F_LOAD_CNC and transition to part-in-CNC.
successCriteria: WIP flags and state reflect unload/load result.
CNC acknowledgement is sent only after load succeeds.
steps.failureBehavior: On timeout or station failure, keep the previous WIP state, stop infeed, set state FAULTED, and raise a CNC-specific alarm.

stateTransition.to: RUNNING_PIPELINE or DRAINING depending on infeed and remaining WIP

externalWaits.onTimeout: Apply global timeout policy. Stop infeed, preserve WIP flags, set lifecycle FAULTED, and report CNC Sync1 timeout.

steps.name: TUBE_INSERTION_EXCHANGE
steps.intent: Unload and/or load the tube insertion station when Sync2 is ready.
preconditions: Tube insertion Sync2 represents a safe process-ready condition.
actions: Go home before tube insertion work.
Wait for DI[105:Sync2] before unload if part is in station.
Wait for DI[105:Sync2] before load if part is staged.
successCriteria: Tube insertion WIP flags and state reflect unload/load result.
Acknowledgement is sent only after load succeeds.
steps.failureBehavior: On timeout or station failure, keep previous WIP state, stop infeed, set state FAULTED, and raise a tube-insertion-specific alarm.

stateTransition.to: RUNNING_PIPELINE or DRAINING depending on infeed and remaining WIP

externalWaits.onTimeout: Apply global timeout policy. Stop infeed, preserve WIP flags, set lifecycle FAULTED, and report tube insertion unload Sync2 timeout.
Apply global timeout policy. Stop infeed, preserve WIP flags, set lifecycle FAULTED, and report tube insertion load Sync2 timeout.

steps.name: CONVEYOR_DROP
steps.intent: Place completed part onto conveyor/drop path and decide whether the cycle should finish.
preconditions: F[65:PART_2_PRINT] is ON for conveyor/drop work.
actions: If infeed and WIP are all clear, finish cycle.
successCriteria: Part-for-conveyor state is cleared by F_CONV_DROP.
Cycle exits only when infeed and WIP state are clear.
steps.failureBehavior: On conveyor/drop failure, preserve WIP state and raise a conveyor/drop-specific alarm.

stateTransition.to: RUNNING_PIPELINE, DRAINING, or FINISHED depending on infeed and remaining WIP flags

steps.name: FINISH_OR_FAULT
steps.intent: Handle terminal alarms and finish-cycle cleanup.
preconditions: Main loop reached a finish condition or an explicit alarm label.
actions: Raise specific UALM for known start/limit/CNC faults.
Re-enable F[60:INFEED] only if reviewed as safe for the terminal condition.
successCriteria: WIP state is preserved when needed for recovery.
steps.failureBehavior: If finish cleanup cannot be proven safe, leave state FAULTED and require operator recovery.

asyncTasks.ownership: Owns vision trigger/retry behavior and writes F[50:Part_Ready], R[103:Retry_Count], and R[105:Hopper_Count].
asyncTasks.singleInstancePolicy: Blocking: define how A_MAIN proves the feeder task is not already running before using RUN.
asyncTasks.statusPolicy: Blocking: F_TASK_STATUS exists but the guard in F_FLEXI_LOADER is commented out; define the status/heartbeat rule before generation.
asyncTasks.stopPolicy: Blocking: define how task exits or closes K_VS connection on STOP, abort, or A_MAIN fault.

asyncTasks.ownership: Owns conveyor running flag F[52] and conveyor outputs during drop/discharge.
asyncTasks.singleInstancePolicy: F_CONV_DROP waits for F[52] OFF before calling F_PLACE_CONVEYOR; A_ version should keep or strengthen this contract.
asyncTasks.statusPolicy: Uses F[52:Conveyor Running] and UALM[10] on timeout.
asyncTasks.stopPolicy: Blocking: define output reset behavior if A_MAIN faults while conveyor task is running.

generation.notes: Planning artifact only. Do not generate A_MAIN LS until blocking review questions are answered.

reviewQuestions.question: Global 3-minute timeout policy cannot control waits inside unchanged legacy F_ routines such as F_UNLOAD_CNC. Convert those routines to A_ before first A_MAIN test, or record a scoped exception.
How should A_MAIN prove F_FLEXI_LOADER/A_FLEXI_LOADER is not already running before starting it?
Which F_ routines may A_MAIN call during wrapper-first migration, and which must be converted to A_ before first robot test?
Confirm $WAITTMOUT is the correct controller wait-timeout variable and confirm its required units/value for a 180-second timeout before LS generation.
